Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1022 upvotes, 145 comments
  2. Defeat as Method 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. The Enterprise Context Layer 27 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. New HyperCard discovery: Neuromancer / Count Zero / Mona Lisa Overdrive 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Google to Discontinue Widevine Cloud License Service in April 2027 24 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 185 upvotes, 66 comments
  7. I used pulsar detection techniques to turn a phone into a watch timegrapher 44 upvotes, 11 comments
  8. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 183 upvotes, 67 comments
  9. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 134 upvotes, 47 comments
  10. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 257 upvotes, 109 comments
  11. Because Algospeak 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. I put my whole life into a single database 367 upvotes, 175 comments
  13. Converting Binary Floating-Point Numbers to Shortest Decimal Strings 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Open Weights Isn't Open Training 39 upvotes, 14 comments
  15. Billion-Parameter Theories 61 upvotes, 27 comments
  16. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  17. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 398 upvotes, 243 comments
  18. Meta acquires Moltbook 281 upvotes, 189 comments
  19. Maybe the G in AGI stands for Gemini 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  20. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 28 upvotes, 12 comments
  21. Rebasing in Magit 150 upvotes, 105 comments
  22. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 205 upvotes, 163 comments
  23. Levels of Agentic Engineering 37 upvotes, 22 comments
  24. You hired the AI to write the tests. Of course they pass 45 upvotes, 32 comments
  25. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 321 upvotes, 325 comments
  26. RFC 454545 – Human Em Dash Standard 98 upvotes, 88 comments
  27. Throwing away 18 months of code and starting over 43 upvotes, 46 comments
  28. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 39 upvotes, 43 comments
  29. I built a programming language using Claude Code 71 upvotes, 90 comments
  30. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 130 upvotes, 257 comments

Last generated 4:00am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
